projects.name: Koan.Data.Connector.Mongo (src/Connectors/Data/Mongo, ~3.3k LOC)
projects.purpose: MongoDB document adapter; the flagship NoSQL connector with full pushdown, GUID identity-encoding (DATA-0098), partition-safe collection caching, native TTL retention.
projects.state: active, first-class — richest DataCaps set in fleet (Linq, BulkUpsert/Delete, AtomicBatch, FastRemove, ConditionalReplace, Retention.TtlIndex); just extended for JOBS-0005 §20 (CAS claim + expireAfterSeconds=0 TTL index at MongoRepository.cs:263-265); careful concurrency comments; zero TODOs. Only adapter declaring TtlIndex. Gap: no Query.String/IRawQueryRepository.

projects.name: Koan.Data.Connector.Postgres (~2.3k LOC)
projects.purpose: PostgreSQL relational adapter (Dapper+Npgsql) with JSONB projection pushdown, relational schema orchestration, and Aspire dev-container provisioning.
projects.state: active, first-class — full RelationalFilterSupport pushdown + ConditionalReplace; covered by FilterConvergence oracle + Jobs adapter suite. Seams: does NOT implement IBulkUpsert / declare DataCaps.Write.BulkUpsert (UpsertMany exists at line 633 but capability unadvertised — parity gap vs Sqlite/SqlServer/Mongo); stale TODO 'Configure proper health check' in KoanAutoRegistrar.cs:168 (Aspire section); only adapter (with Redis) taking hard Aspire.Hosting package deps; known pre-existing ToRowOptimized ordering bug (backlog).

projects.name: Koan.Data.Connector.Sqlite (~2.6k LOC)
projects.purpose: SQLite relational adapter for local dev/test and persistent L1 scenarios; same relational orchestration stack as Postgres/SqlServer.
keyTypes: SqliteRepository<TEntity,TKey> (1732 LOC — largest single file in the fleet; full interface set incl. IConditionalWriteRepository)
projects.state: active, first-class — identical cap set to SqlServer (Linq, String, BulkUpsert/Delete, AtomicBatch, FastRemove, ConditionalReplace, RelationalFilterSupport.Default); Jobs+Cache adapter suites ride it. Code smell: 1732-line repository monolith (DDL, instructions, query, batch all inline). No Orchestration/ evaluator (correct — file-based, nothing to containerize).

projects.name: Koan.Data.Connector.SqlServer (~1.8k LOC)
projects.purpose: SQL Server relational adapter with JSON-projection pushdowns; mirrors Sqlite/Postgres shape.
keyTypes: SqlServerRepository<TEntity,TKey> (926 LOC; full interface set incl. IConditionalWriteRepository)
projects.state: active, first-class — cap parity with Sqlite; MARS/DDL bugs recently fixed via FilterConvergence oracle; TECHNICAL.md documents NotSupported-predicate policy. Has Discovery but no Orchestration evaluator and no Aspire wiring (asymmetric vs Postgres); known backlog: orchestrator-columns item.

projects.name: Koan.Data.Connector.Couchbase (~2.9k LOC)
projects.purpose: Couchbase document adapter: N1QL pushdown, KV durability levels, real cluster transactions, heaviest readiness machinery in the fleet.
projects.state: active but lagging the capability ladder — declares String/Linq/BulkUpsert/BulkDelete/AtomicBatch + N1QL filter caps but NOT FastRemove, ConditionalReplace, or TtlIndex (skipped by both DATA-0083 and the JOBS-0005 §20 wave; per 'no stopgaps' rule these should be implemented, not floor-dropped). Tests exist (CRUD + FilterConvergence) but RequireDocker-gated with a known Testcontainers-networking backlog item, so it is the least empirically verified of the five real DBs.

projects.name: Koan.Data.Connector.Redis (~1.4k LOC)
projects.purpose: Redis key/value adapter: string-key CRUD, full-keyspace SCAN queries with client-side filtering, Aspire dev-container wiring.
keyTypes: KoanAutoRegistrar (302 LOC — larger than the repository itself)
projects.state: active build but neglected tier — caps are only Linq (in-memory) + FastRemove + FilterSupport.Full, yet 'Full' is honored by ScanAll(page:1,size:int.MaxValue) + InMemoryFilterEvaluator (RedisRepository.cs:85-90): materialize-everything semantics identical to Json/InMemory, dangerous at scale. No bulk caps, AtomicBatch throws NotSupportedException (line 284), no conditional write, no TTL despite Redis being the natural TTL store. Stale TODO at KoanAutoRegistrar.cs:277. Tests: a single Capabilities spec + fixture — thinnest coverage in the fleet.

projects.name: Koan.Data.Connector.ElasticSearch (~1.4k LOC)
projects.purpose: Elasticsearch VECTOR adapter (kNN, index provisioning, metadata filtering via REST) — despite living under Connectors/Data it implements IVectorSearchRepository, not the document-store surface.
projects.state: active — covered by VectorAdapterSurface TestKit suite; but it is an ~80%-identical twin of OpenSearch (normalized diff of the two repositories is 258 lines out of 1295) and is misfiled relative to src/Connectors/Data/Vector/* (Weaviate/Qdrant/Milvus/PGVector).

projects.name: Koan.Data.Connector.OpenSearch (~1.3k LOC)
projects.purpose: OpenSearch vector adapter; structural clone of the ElasticSearch connector over the shared Koan.Data.SearchEngine translator.
projects.state: active — own VectorAdapterSurface test suite; same misfiling and twin-code observation as ElasticSearch (the DATA-0097 SearchEngine extraction got the filter translator but stopped before the repository body).

projects.name: Koan.Data.Connector.Json (~0.6k LOC)
projects.purpose: JSON-file-backed repository for demos/tests/local dev; one file per entity store, client-side filtering.
keyTypes: JsonRepository<TEntity,TKey> (332 LOC; Linq + FilterSupport.Full, no batch — AtomicBatch throws)
projects.state: active, deliberately minimal dev-tier — recently touched by the FilterConvergence oracle (default-sort fix). Carries three registration surfaces (KoanAutoRegistrar, JsonAdapterRegistration, AddJsonData extension) plus the JsonRepo escape hatch — surface bloat for a demo adapter.

projects.name: Koan.Data.Connector.InMemory (~0.4k LOC)
projects.purpose: Thread-safe in-memory store (per entity-type × partition ConcurrentDictionary) for testing and the bottom rung of every capability ladder (DATA-0081).
keyTypes: InMemoryRepository<TEntity,TKey> (242 LOC; Linq, BulkUpsert/Delete, AtomicBatch, FilterSupport.Full)
projects.state: active, clean, smallest and best-proportioned project in the area; broadest spec set of the dev-tier adapters (CRUD/Batch/Caps/Filtering/Instructions/Partition/Sorting). No health contributor (correct — nothing external). Notably declares AtomicBatch while Redis/Json don't.

cohesion: Strong as a pillar: all 10 follow one canonical anatomy (Repository + AdapterFactory + Options/OptionsConfigurator + HealthContributor + Infrastructure/Constants + Initialization/KoanAutoRegistrar + Discovery adapter + TECHNICAL.md/README), all flow through the same DataCaps/FilterSupport capability model (ARCH-0084) and the same QueryDefinition/filter-AST pushdown pipeline (DATA-0096/0099/0100). The relational trio (Sqlite/Postgres/SqlServer) additionally shares IRelationalSchemaOrchestrator from Koan.Data.Relational. Fractures: (1) ElasticSearch/OpenSearch are vector adapters parked in the document-connector directory while a sibling Vector/ directory holds the other four vector adapters — the taxonomy lies; (2) capability declaration is uneven where it shouldn't be (Postgres ships UpsertMany but doesn't advertise BulkUpsert; Couchbase missed the ConditionalReplace/FastRemove/Ttl waves; Redis advertises FilterSupport.Full for what is a full-keyspace scan + client-side filter); (3) orchestration/Aspire integration is inconsistent — Postgres and Redis embed Aspire.Hosting packages and resource-builder code in their registrars (with matching stale TODOs), Mongo/Couchbase have evaluator-only orchestration, SqlServer/Sqlite have neither; (4) every adapter has BOTH a manual XRegistration class and a KoanAutoRegistrar registering the same services — two registration paths per adapter × 10 adapters; (5) Mongo uniquely couples to Koan.ZenGarden.Core via an offering binding (with both 'mongo' and 'mongodb' alias ids), the only cross-pillar reference in the fleet.
redundancies: SchemaDdlPolicy.cs + SchemaMatchingMode.cs are byte-identical (namespace aside) in Sqlite/, Postgres/, SqlServer/ — three copies of two public enums that belong in Koan.Data.Relational.

ElasticSearchVectorRepository (704 LOC) vs OpenSearchVectorRepository (591 LOC): normalized diff is 258/1295 lines (~80% identical); factories, options configurators (189 LOC each), health contributors, and Initialization registrars are likewise parallel — the shared Koan.Data.SearchEngine project only captured the filter translator.

Dual registration path per adapter: e.g. MongoRegistration.cs:23 and Initialization/KoanAutoRegistrar.cs:59 both TryAddEnumerable the same MongoHealthContributor (same pattern in Couchbase/Postgres/Sqlite/SqlServer/Json) — every service is wired twice in code.

Redis/Json/InMemory all implement Query() as materialize-all + InMemoryFilterEvaluator.Compile + InMemorySorter — three hand-rolled copies of the same client-side evaluation loop that could be one shared 'naive store' base.

Per-adapter Telemetry shims (MongoTelemetry, CouchbaseTelemetry, SqliteTelemetry, PgTelemetry, SqlServerTelemetry, ElasticSearchTelemetry, OpenSearchTelemetry) — seven near-identical ActivitySource holders.

Mongo carries three init units (KoanAutoRegistrar, MongoOptimizationAutoRegistrar, MongoRegistration) plus a local SimpleOptionsMonitor shim; the optimization registrar's process-global driver mutation under a static lock is a second bootstrap idiom inside one adapter.
Json exposes three+ registration surfaces (KoanAutoRegistrar, JsonAdapterRegistration, AddJsonData<T,K> extension, JsonRepo static factory) for a demo-tier adapter.
deadOrExperimental: src/Connectors/Data/Cqrs/Outbox/Mongo (Koan.Data.Cqrs.Outbox.Connector.Mongo, ~344 LOC, sibling to the in-scope ten): in Koan.sln but zero hits in tests/ and samples/ — vestigial CQRS-outbox scaffold predating the Jobs transactional outbox; tombstone candidate.
Json/JsonRepo.cs static DI-bypass factory — dev convenience with no framework consumers found; cut candidate.

Stale TODO blocks in Aspire sections: Postgres/Initialization/KoanAutoRegistrar.cs:168 and Redis/Initialization/KoanAutoRegistrar.cs:277 ('Configure proper health check' with commented-out WithHealthCheck) — abandoned Aspire health wiring.
Mongo/Initialization/MongoZenGardenOfferingBinding.cs dual 'mongo'/'mongodb' alias bindings — smells like a compatibility shim awaiting a canonical adapter-id decision.

Redis adapter as a *data* connector is borderline experimental in practice: one test spec, scan-all queries, no batch/TTL — its real production role in the repo is the Cache/Coherence adapter (separate area), not entity storage.
consolidationCandidates: Merge ElasticSearch + OpenSearch into one Koan.Data.SearchEngine-based connector with a small dialect seam (auth header, distance-metric names, knn query shape): repositories are ~80% identical and the shared translator already exists; saves ~1.3k LOC and one whole project. While at it, relocate the result under src/Connectors/Data/Vector/ (or rename the package from Data.Connector.* to Vector.*) so the directory taxonomy matches reality.

Hoist SchemaDdlPolicy/SchemaMatchingMode (and probably the orchestrator-invocation boilerplate around IRelationalSchemaOrchestrator + per-adapter DdlExecutor lookup, repeated at SqliteRepository.cs:225/276/899/981 and SqlServerRepository.cs:179/680/689) into Koan.Data.Relational — three adapters re-declare the same governance enums and the same EnsureCreated choreography.

Collapse the dual registration path: with ARCH-0086 KoanModule landing, fold each adapter's XRegistration class into its KoanAutoRegistrar/module so there is exactly one wiring unit per adapter (10 classes deleted, removes double-registration risk).

Extract a shared 'materialized store' base (scan + InMemoryFilterEvaluator + InMemorySorter + paging) consumed by Redis/Json/InMemory; Redis then only contributes KV I/O. Alternatively decide Redis is not an entity store, demote/cut Koan.Data.Connector.Redis, and keep Redis as cache/coherence only — its single-spec test coverage suggests nobody dogfoods it as data.

Close the capability-parity gaps instead of carrying asymmetry: Postgres should declare/implement IBulkUpsert (UpsertMany already exists); Couchbase should get ConditionalReplace + FastRemove (it has real transactions, so CAS is implementable) or an ADR note saying why not — currently it silently misses the JOBS-0005 §20 ladder; Redis should either gain native TTL/SCAN-cursor paging or stop advertising FilterSupport.Full.

Move Aspire resource-provisioning out of Postgres/Redis registrars into the orchestration layer (Koan.Orchestration.Aspire) so adapters don't take Aspire.Hosting.* package deps — restores the layering the other 8 adapters already follow and deletes the stale TODO blocks.

Decouple Mongo from Koan.ZenGarden.Core: the IZenGardenOfferingBinding should be discovered/registered from the ZenGarden side (or via a neutral manifest), not a project reference inside a data adapter — it is the only cross-pillar edge in the fleet.
Tombstone src/Connectors/Data/Cqrs/Outbox/Mongo (superseded by the Jobs-tier transactional outbox; no consumers).

Split SqliteRepository.cs (1732 lines) and PostgresRepository.cs (1147 lines) along the seams the other adapters already use (DDL/schema, instructions, query translation) — same behavior, files under the 800-line ceiling.

maturitySignals.tests: Best-covered area in the repo. tests/Suites/Data has a per-connector suite for 8 of 10 (Couchbase, InMemory, Json, Mongo, Postgres, Redis, Sqlite, SqlServer) plus shared canon kits: Koan.Data.AdapterSurface.TestKit (FilterConvergence + TemporalConvergence oracles, consumed by Couchbase/InMemory/Mongo/Postgres/Sqlite/SqlServer — NOT Json/Redis) and Koan.Data.VectorAdapterSurface.TestKit (ElasticSearch/OpenSearch suites). Cross-cutting: Koan.Data.Filtering.Tests, Koan.Data.Convergence.Tests, comparable-encoding specs (DATA-0100) on real Mongo/PG/SqlServer/SQLite. Adapters are additionally exercised by Jobs (Sqlite/Postgres/Mongo/SqlServer), Cache (Sqlite/Redis), and Web AdapterSurface (7 adapters) suites. Weak spots: Redis = one Capabilities spec only; Couchbase suites Docker-gated with a known Testcontainers-networking backlog; ES/OS verified only via vector surface.
maturitySignals.docs: Uniformly good at the project level: every adapter ships README.md + TECHNICAL.md (TECHNICAL docs state pushdown/NotSupported policies explicitly). Deep ADR trail in docs/decisions (DATA-0081 InMemory, 0083 FastRemove, 0086 naming, 0088 auto-config resolver, 0092/0093 sort, 0094 partitions, 0096 unified filter, 0098 identity encoding, 0099/0100 convergence/encoding). Gap: no single adapter×capability matrix doc (no 'adapter-matrix' found in docs/guides or docs/reference); docs/guides/data has only two streaming/access guides — the capability story lives in code (Describe) and scattered ADRs.
maturitySignals.codeQuality: High and recently worked: only 2 stale TODOs in the whole in-scope fleet (both in abandoned Aspire health-check blocks, Postgres:168/Redis:277); NotSupportedException usage is deliberate and documented policy, not scaffolding; rich intent comments (e.g. MongoRepository partition-race rationale, §20.4 TTL note, SharpCompress/Snappier CVE-pin comments in csproj). Naming is consistent (X{Repository,AdapterFactory,Options,OptionsConfigurator,HealthContributor,Telemetry}). Debt is structural, not hygienic: 1732-line SqliteRepository, copy-pasted enums in the relational trio, ~80%-twin ES/OS repositories, dual registration classes per adapter, and capability declarations drifting from actual behavior at the edges (Postgres BulkUpsert, Redis FilterSupport.Full-via-ScanAll).
